article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

sonnet_review.biasedText: The article prominently features Hills Christian Community School's AI innovations across multiple paragraphs and includes a photograph and quotes from its leader of digital innovation, while alternative perspectives on potential downsides or failures of AI implementation are relegated to brief mentions near the end.
sonnet_review.reason: This aligns with my own detection of slant. The article heavily favors positive AI adoption framing while condensing critical perspectives into minimal coverage. Sonnet correctly identifies the structural imbalance, though the 'biasedText' is a description rather than a direct quote from the article.
Matches my own detection exactly. 'Brand-new' is a subjective promotional qualifier applied to technologies that are not necessarily new.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The article extensively quotes the chief executive of Independent Schools Australia and features a private independent school's AI program, while public school teachers' concerns are summarized only through aggregate survey data.
sonnet_review.reason: This is a defensible observation. The article does privilege institutional voices (ISA chief executive, school innovation leader) over rank-and-file teachers, whose concerns are only referenced through survey data. While I didn't flag this specifically, the asymmetry in sourcing is real and represents a form of source selection bias.

gpt_review.biasedText:
At Hills Christian Community School in the Adelaide Hills, the technology is just one way teachers and students are using artificial intelligence and other brand-new tech to further learning.
Students also use sensors, drones and coding to learn about natural ecosystems, from rivers to pollinators and bushland habitats. Students with disabilities, including limited speech, are accessing Meta AI glasses with inbuilt speakers that explain what is happening without disrupting the classroom.
gpt_review.reason: Aligns with my detection of slant. The article's structure foregrounds positive examples at length while minimizing critical perspectives.

gpt_review.biasedText: at Australian schools in innovative ways
gpt_review.reason: I didn't flag this specific instance separately, but 'innovative' is indeed a subjective positive characterization by the author. It's a defensible detection of loaded word choice.

gpt_review.biasedText: The latest Teaching and Learning International Survey (Talis), released in 2024, found two-thirds of Australian teachers in secondary years and just under half of primary school teachers used AI in their work, placing the nation among countries with the highest uptake of the technology.
gpt_review.reason: This is reporting of survey data. The framing 'placing the nation among countries with the highest uptake' is factual context from the survey, not editorializing by the author. Reporting statistics about adoption rates is standard journalism, and the article does follow this with teachers expressing caution. This is a stretch.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: an AI chatbot will interrogate them about it: put them on the spot in a two-way dialogue
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the sensationalized word choices 'interrogate' and 'put them on the spot' which inject a confrontational tone into what is described as a guided questioning process.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: It's not just about hammering in the lesson. It's also a way to ensure students do their own thinking, and haven't resorted to plagiarism or ChatGPT.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the author presenting as unattributed fact that the AI tool 'ensures' students do their own thinking and haven't plagiarized — a contested claim about effectiveness presented without evidence or attribution.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: an AI chatbot will interrogate them about it: put them on the spot in a two-way dialogue
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the dramatized characterization using 'interrogate' and 'put them on the spot' which sensationalize what is a guided questioning process.
GPT did not specifically flag 'brand-new' as a subjective qualifier, though it caught 'innovative' as word choice. 'Brand-new' is arguably more clearly promotional.

gpt_false_negatives.biasedText: It's not just about hammering in the lesson. It's also a way to ensure students do their own thinking, and haven't resorted to plagiarism or ChatGPT.
gpt_false_negatives.reason: GPT missed the author presenting contested claims about AI tool effectiveness as established fact without attribution.
